content: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced in October 2025 when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

content: Fannie Masemola is accused of failing in his duties to provide proper oversight in his role.

content: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

title: Myanmar’s military government rebuffed on peace talks offer
content: Min Aung Hlaing elected by parliament as president earlier this month after election derided as a sham.

content: Legal aid group says the rate of requests shows pro-Palestine advocacy persists despite Trump administration's tactics.

content: More than four in every 10 deaths and disappearances occurred on sea routes to Europe, UN agency says.

content: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
content: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

originalTitle: How Leicester went from Premier League champions to League One in a decade
title: How Leicester went from Premier League champions to League One in a decade
content: Ten years ago, Leicester were days away from winning the Premier League. Next season, they will play in League One. How?

content: Brighton heap more pressure on Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior as they ease to a comfortable 3-0 win thanks to goals from Ferdi Kadioglu, Jack Hinshelwood and Danny Welbeck.

originalTitle: Players can celebrate how they want - Guardiola
title: Players can celebrate how they want - Guardiola
content: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his players can "celebrate however you want" in response to criticism for their reaction to beating Arsenal.
